item.title: Learning Where and When: Patch-Based Spatiotemporal Localization in Weakly Supervised Video Anomaly Detection
item.content:encoded: Weakly supervised video anomaly detection (WSVAD) has predominantly focused on temporal localization, identifying when anomalies occur while largely neglecting their spatial extent within frames. Yet, spatial localization is essential for interpretability and practical deployment in real-world settings. We introduce a patch-based spatiotemporal framework for weakly supervised anomaly localization that jointly models where and when anomalies occur. Our approach operates on grid-level patch features and learns region-level anomaly scores under a multiple instance learning paradigm. We further propose a Proximity-Aware Top-k spatiotemporal selection strategy that enables the model to generate fine-grained spatial anomaly maps without requiring bounding-box supervision during training. Our method surpasses existing state-of-the-art approaches across multiple benchmarks, yielding substantial gains in spatiotemporal localization accuracy. In addition, we release frame-level bounding-box annotations for the test sets of two widely used datasets, along with our code and pretrained models, providing new resources to facilitate future research in spatially grounded WSVAD.

item.title: Separating Expert Retention from Autonomous Source Inference in Raw-ECG-Replay-Free Continual ECG Deployment
item.content:encoded: In multi-source ECG deployment, models may need to incorporate new data sources when earlier raw ECGs cannot be retained or replayed. Freezing a pretrained backbone and assigning each source an isolated classifier prevents parameter interference, but deployment still requires selecting an expert when source metadata are unavailable. We study this distinction through \ours{}, an incremental expert bank built on frozen 1024-dimensional ECGFounder features. Each arriving domain adds a balanced-softmax linear expert, while a lightweight router is fitted only on retained training features and domain labels from sources observed so far. A validation-calibrated margin rule fuses the two most likely experts instead of committing to a single routed expert. On CPSC, PTB-XL, Georgia, and Chapman-Shaoxing, source-aware expert selection reaches $0.7915\pm0.0036$ Macro-F1 and a matched offline independent-head reference reaches $0.7885\pm0.0009$, supporting strong source-aware expert retention. Without source IDs, an MLP router reaches $0.7756\pm0.0027$ and top-2 margin fusion reaches $0.7782\pm0.0022$. The top-2 gain over hard MLP routing is small ($+0.0026$), with a 95\% confidence interval from paired bootstrap that includes zero. Across three domain orders, the top-2-to-oracle gap remains $0.0111$--$0.0133$, identifying autonomous source inference as the main remaining bottleneck. No raw ECGs are replayed, but frozen training features are retained for router updates; the method is therefore not memory-free.

item.title: Beyond Triplet Plausibility: Relation Set Completion in Knowledge Graphs
item.content:encoded: Knowledge graphs (KGs) organize real-world knowledge as triplets and underpin many downstream applications. Due to their inherent incompleteness, knowledge graph completion (KGC) is widely studied and is typically formulated as triplet prediction, with link prediction as the dominant paradigm. However, this formulation focuses on the incompleteness of triplet-wise information and overlooks the incompleteness of entity-relation compatibility information. To address this limitation, we introduce a relation set completion task (RSC), which complements the link prediction task and aims to reason about missing relations that are semantically compatible with a given entity. We further propose a Relation Set Embedding model (RelSetE), which models latent patterns among the observed relations of entities to infer missing ones. To evaluate RelSetE, we derive three benchmark datasets from standard KG benchmarks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RelSetE effectively captures entity-relation compatibility patterns and performs favorably in inferring missing relations of entities. Code and data are publicly available.

item.title: The Wiola Architecture for Efficient Small Language Models
item.content:encoded: We present Wiola, a fully original Small Language Model (SLM) architecture built from first principles, sharing no structural lineage with any existing model family including GPT, LLaMA, Mistral, or Falcon. Wiola introduces five independently novel components: (i) Spiral Rotary Positional Encoding (SRPE), which embeds token positions on a three-dimensional helical manifold combining absolute, relative, and hierarchical positional signals; (ii) Gated Cross-Layer Attention (GCLA), providing each decoder layer with soft cross-attention access to compressed summaries of two preceding layers for inter-layer coherence; (iii) Adaptive Token Merging (ATM), which dynamically merges se mantically redundant adjacent tokens in middle network layers to reduce attention complexity without information loss; (iv) Dual Stream Feed-Forward (DSFF), replacing the conventional MLP with two parallel streams fused by a learned per-dimension gate; and (v) WiolaRMSNorm, a modified normalisation introducing a per-dimension learned offset vector that prevents representation collapse. We provide complete mathematical derivations, architectural block diagrams, complexity analyses, and systematic comparisons against GPT-2, LLaMA-2, and Mistral. Wiola is released in four sizes (120M, 360M, 700M, and 1.5B parameters) and is fully compatible with the HuggingFace Transformers ecosystem, with all 22 architectural unit tests passing.

item.title: The Joint Effect of Quantization and Sampling Temperature on LLM Safety Alignment: A Factorial Analysis
item.content:encoded: Modern LLM deployments routinely compress models and raise sampling temperature to reduce cost, latency, or repetition, yet safety evaluations usually treat these choices as fixed implementation details. This leaves a practical uncertainty: does a model that is safe at FP16 and greedy decoding remain safe after it is quantized and sampled stochastically, or do the two deployment knobs amplify one another? We study this question with a factorial evaluation of 9 instruction-tuned models from six families, 3 precisions (FP16, GPTQ INT8, AWQ INT4), and 6 temperatures ($T{=}0$ to $1.0$), yielding 161 configurations and $\approx$322k responses judged by a six-model safety ensemble. Contrary to the concern that low-bit deployment broadly erodes alignment, standard non-adversarial quantization is usually safety-neutral: INT4 keeps or lowers attack success for 7 of 9 models, with clear degradation concentrated in the weakest baseline model, SmolLM3-3B ($18.5\%{\to}36.0\%$). The larger risk comes from sampling: higher temperature sharply increases decision instability for vulnerable models, with DFR reaching 53.0\% at $T{=}1.0$, even when average ASR changes modestly. Finally, the interaction is not a ``double penalty'': our Compound Degradation Index remains largely sub-additive ($-0.195$ to $+0.045$), indicating that quantization and temperature do not systematically compound. These results suggest a deployment rule of thumb: standard INT4/INT8 quantization can be reasonable for strongly aligned models, but safety claims at elevated temperature should report multi-sample stability, not only average attack success.

item.title: Cognitive World Models for Process-Level Social Influence Evaluation
item.content:encoded: Social influence dialogue changes user behavior by altering internal cognitive states. The central evaluation question is whether the user's beliefs, desires, intentions, and emotions measurably change over the course of conversation, a process-oriented criterion that neither surface-level text metrics (BLEU/ROUGE) nor single-score LLM judgments can capture. We propose the \textbf{Cog}nitive \textbf{W}orld \textbf{M}odel \textbf{(CogWM)}, an LLM-based user model that reframes multi-turn dialogue evaluation from ``what did the user say'' to ``how did the user's internal cognitive state evolves.'' CogWM jointly predicts BDI/E cognitive states and user utterances and serves as both a user simulator and an evaluation platform, using a three-tier evaluation framework that covers turn-level fidelity, trajectory-level state dynamics, and task-level composite scoring. Trained via our \textbf{S}ummarize-\textbf{a}nd-\textbf{A}llocate \textbf{(SaA)} annotation pipeline on 150,454 user-turn samples across four social influence scenarios, CogWM achieves 77.6\% emotion accuracy (2.1$\times$ over GPT-5.5). In 3600 multi-agent discrimination trials, it distinguishes six commercial agents by their cognitive influence, with Llama-4-Scout ranking first (CTS +0.233). CogWM moves social influence dialogue evaluation from terminal judgment to process tracking. We have released our code\footnote{\scriptsize Code: https://github.com/lucianma05-create/CogWM} and models\footnote{Model: https://www.modelscope.cn/models/LucianMa/CogWM-14B}.

item.title: Risk Architecture for AI-Native Engineering Teams: An Organizational Framework for Agentic System Governance
item.content:encoded: Engineering management research has produced mature frameworks for software risk: ownership by feature, escalation by severity, and assurance by test coverage. These frameworks implicitly assume deterministic behavior, discrete and auditable change events, and clear component-to-owner mappings. Teams that build and operate agentic AI systems violate all three assumptions at once: outputs are probabilistic, systems take autonomous multi-step actions, and the risk surface mutates silently between deployments. Existing AI risk literature addresses this from above (policy frameworks such as the NIST AI RMF and ISO/IEC 42001) or below (threat taxonomies such as OWASP's agentic AI guidance), but not at the layer where an engineering manager (EM) operates: roles, decision rights, and escalation structures. This paper contributes (i) a seven-dimension profile distinguishing pure software-engineering, hybrid, and AI-native teams; (ii) a six-cluster failure-mode taxonomy including a previously unarticulated cluster, dependency-boundary determinism mismatch; and (iii) a synthetic framework-adequacy methodology scoring how well each profile's risk architecture detects, contains, and escalates a defined scenario set. Because the object of study is framework adequacy rather than human behavior, the evaluation yields derived rather than observed coverage claims. Coverage degrades as teams move from pure software engineering to AI-native operation, monotonically in the median and abruptly in the count of uncovered, high-consequence failures appearing only at the AI-native step. The degradation concentrates in specific failure-mode categories, and the most severe, least-covered failures arise not inside AI-native teams but at the organizational boundary where their probabilistic outputs are consumed by determinism-assuming dependencies.

item.title: Miti360: A Comprehensive Dataset for Improved Reforestation Monitoring
item.content:encoded: Over the past decade, interest in applying machine learning (ML) to automate forest monitoring has grown significantly. However, existing training datasets are predominantly drawn from North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia, leaving a critical gap in African forestry data. To address this limited geographic diversity, we present Miti360, a comprehensive dataset for reforestation monitoring that comprises high-resolution imagery, ground truth data, and longitudinal weather data. Data collection occurred within a 770-ha reforested section of the Kieni Forest in Kenya between March 2023 and February 2025. Miti360 comprises aerial photos (orthophotos and tiles) with tree bounding box annotations, terrestrial images (single and stereo), and detailed data records including tree biophysical parameters, species, and GPS coordinates, alongside historical weather data. Aerial surveys utilized a DJI Mavic 2 Pro, with imagery stitched via Agisoft Metashape and tiled using ArcGIS Pro, while terrestrial captures used smartphones and custom stereo cameras. Miti360 enables the training of ML systems for tasks such as accelerating tree censuses, matching species to geographical areas, modelling growth based on weather conditions, and developing digital twin frameworks. Models can be trained on Miti360 to address challenges specific to Sub-Saharan Africa, ultimately advancing reforestation monitoring and fostering sustainable forestry practices in underrepresented regions. We demonstrate the utility of this dataset by successfully tracking tree crowns across three years and improving the DeepForest model's box precision and box recall by 12% and 69% respectively through fine-tuning on Miti360.

item.title: Beyond Pixel Overlap: A Framework for Decomposing Segmentation Evaluation Metrics
item.content:encoded: Evaluation metrics are central to binary target segmentation because they determine how progress is measured, compared, and interpreted. In this paper, target denotes the task-defined positive region to be segmented rather than a generic foreground object. It may be salient, camouflaged, transparent, glass-like, mirror-like, shadow-like, lesion-like, or defined by other application-specific semantics. We treat existing metrics as compositions of modular design choices rather than isolated formulas. The proposed framework decomposes each metric into five stages covering prediction representation, target extraction, target matching, score computation, and metric reporting. We use this framework to analyze representative metrics and show how newer metrics address specific limits in earlier protocols. The stage choices keep each metric's assumptions visible. We then discuss the design space opened by the framework and its implications for task-aware evaluation protocols. Reference code is available at https://github.com/lartpang/PySODMetrics.

item.title: LuxEmo: Expressive Text-to-Speech Corpus for Luxembourgish
item.content:encoded: State-of-the-art speech datasets predominantly focus on widely spoken languages, often overlooking low-resource languages such as Luxembourgish, which remain underrepresented in speech technology research. In this work, we introduce LuxEmo, a 21-hour conversational expressive speech corpus for Luxembourgish with 4 emotion categories. LuxEmo is derived from Radio Télévision Luxembourg (RTL) youth broadcasts, using automated detection followed by human validation. We propose a semi-automatic curation workflow combining voice activity detection, denoising, language identification, LuxASR-based segmentation, automatic emotion prediction, lexical cues, and targeted human review. Additionally, we benchmark five expressive TTS systems covering German-based cross-lingual transfer, multilingual Luxembourgish support, Luxembourgish adaptation, and non-parametric prosody transfer. Performance is evaluated using both objective metrics and human evaluation.

item.title: EvLIR: Learning Illumination Residuals from Ordered Events for Low-Light Image Enhancement
item.content:encoded: Low-light image enhancement is severely ill-posed when the input frame contains missing structure, saturated noise, and weak local contrast. Event cameras provide asynchronous brightness-change observations with high temporal resolution, but prior works often treat voxel channels as an unordered or static feature stack before fusion, rather than explicitly modeling their within-window temporal evolution, weakening the temporal evidence that makes events useful. We propose EvLIR, a temporal-residual enhancement framework that learns illumination residuals from ordered events for low-light image enhancement. Given a low-light frame and its aligned event voxel, EvLIR preserves the ordered temporal bins of the event stream and introduces a Temporal Event Residual Module (TERM) to encode short-window event dynamics with a lightweight ConvGRU. The resulting temporal state is converted into a bounded illumination correction, which provides spatially adaptive photometric guidance for Retinex-style illumination estimation and subsequent reliability-aware image-event restoration. On SDE and SDSD indoor/outdoor benchmarks, EvLIR achieves the best result on eleven of twelve dataset-metric pairs, with average scores of 25.63~dB PSNR, 28.30~dB PSNR*, and 0.827 SSIM across the four benchmarks.

item.title: DEEPMED Search: An Open-Source Agentic Platform for Medical Deep Research with Introspective Verification
item.content:encoded: Navigating the deluge of heterogeneous medical data, from academic literature (PubMed) to clinical guidelines (Web) and private knowledge bases, remains a critical bottleneck for evidence-based medicine. While commercial black-box tools lack transparency, standard open-source RAG implementations frequently suffer from reasoning drift when handling complex, long-tail queries. We present DEEPMED Search, a fully open-source, agentic platform designed for transparent medical deep research. Built on a high-performance Next.js architecture, DEEPMED Search features a source-adaptive router that autonomously dispatches sub-queries to PubMed, web search, or local graph-based knowledge bases based on information density. Crucially, the platform integrates an introspective verification module, powered by a causal-consistent multi-agent debate framework, to validate retrieved evidence against diagnostic logic before synthesis. To demonstrate its robustness, we showcase DEEPMED Search's ability to autonomously decompose high-difficulty rare disease queries, filter out confounding noise, and generate structured, citation-backed research reports in minutes. By open-sourcing this software, we provide the community with a robust infrastructure to democratize access to trustworthy, glass-box medical reasoning in research and prototyping settings.

item.title: SimpleSearch-VL: A Simple Recipe for Multimodal Agentic Deep Search
item.content:encoded: We present SimpleSearch-VL, an efficient, reliable, and practical framework for multimodal agentic search. Its core idea is to improve the agent's own search-and-verification process rather than scaling data, tools, or auxiliary model components. For efficiency, Factorized Adaptive Rollout (FAR) improves sampling efficiency by forming more informative training groups while using redundant samples to mitigate long-tail latency and expose hard samples. For reliability, SimpleSearch-VL performs evidence-verified reasoning, explicitly using chain-of-thought verification to assess the relevance of retrieved visual and textual cues to the original context. For practicality, SimpleSearch-VL keeps a lightweight tool interface and performs webpage self-summary within the agent, requiring no additional external dependencies. With only 5K supervised tool-interleaved trajectories and 2K RL data, SimpleSearch-VL improves Qwen3-VL agentic baselines by 15.8 and 16.0 average points for the 8B and 30B-A3B variants, respectively. The SimpleSearch-VL-30B-A3B model further achieves performance competitive with agentic Gemini-3-Pro.

item.title: Decentralized Stochastic Subgradient-type Methods with Communication Compression for Nonsmooth Nonconvex Optimization
item.content:encoded: In this paper, we consider the nonsmooth nonconvex decentralized optimization problem, where inter-agent communication is compressed. We propose a general framework that unifies various decentralized stochastic subgradient-type methods with unbiased compression and contractive compression with error compensation. By relating the consensus-error iterates and the averaged iterates to the trajectories of continuous-time differential inclusions, we establish global convergence for all methods encompassed by our framework when the objective functions are nonsmooth and lack Clarke regularity. Based on our framework, we further develop several compression-based methods, including decentralized stochastic subgradient methods utilizing sign-based regularization and gradient-tracking momentum. Preliminary numerical experiments empirically support our theoretical results and highlight the communication-accuracy trade-off of the newly developed methods.

item.title: Anti-Collapse Dynamics and the Emergence of Multi-Time-Scale Learning in Recurrent Neural Networks
item.content:encoded: Long-range learning is hard for recurrent networks trained with stochastic gradient descent, because the influence of a past input fades with the lag $\ell$, and if it fades too fast the dependence cannot be learned from finite data. This fade is captured by an envelope $f(\ell)$. An exponential fade makes the data needed to learn a lag-$\ell$ dependence grow exponentially, putting long horizons out of reach; a power-law fade keeps the cost polynomial. We show that the asymptotic decay class of $f(\ell)$ is not fixed by the architecture. Instead, it emerges from the coupling between the state dynamics and parameter dynamics, settling into either a collapsed regime (fast, exponential forgetting) or an extended, anti-collapsed regime (slow, power-law forgetting). The intuition is a competition within these coupled dynamics. Training drives the network's effective time scales toward short ones, while rare, heavy-tailed fluctuations of the learning dynamics push a few of them to very long values. The extended regime survives only when these heavy-tailed pushes are strong enough to balance the pull. We make this mathematically precise with a coarse-grained stochastic process and prove exactly when the extended regime exists. A single exponent, the spectral exponent~$β$, then governs both the spread of time scales and how slowly the network forgets. Realizing the regime in practice needs one more ingredient: the joint action of the architecture and the optimizer must be able to hold such a broad spread. A network whose capacity to generate broad time-scale spectra is severely constrained still collapses, even when supplied with strong heavy-tailed forcing. Heavy-tailed fluctuations thus act not as noise to be suppressed, but as the mechanism that sustains long-range learning.

item.title: RefineSplat：一种基于熵感知的动态遮罩方法
item.content:encoded:
RefineSplat通过熵感知方法有效识别并消除模糊干扰物。



摘要： 本文提出RefineSplat，一种系统性框架，用于构建动态遮罩以识别多种模糊干扰物。通过定性和定量分析问题，作者提出了基于熵感知的自适应遮罩方法，能够有效区分动态元素与静态场景，特别是在颜色或语义模糊的情况下。此外，还引入了一种简单的熵感知密度控制策略，以在模糊场景中对齐高斯分布。为验证方法有效性，研究者创建并发布了Ambiguous wild数据集，包含18个难以区分干扰物和静态场景的场景。实验结果表明，RefineSplat在多个数据集上表现出色，实现了无干扰的新视角合成。

item.title: 多角色评分框架提升大语言模型评估与优化
item.content:encoded:
MRRG框架通过多角色评分提升大语言模型的奖励信号和偏好评估。



摘要： 本文提出一种无需训练和参考的多角色评分生成框架（MRRG），通过从多个互补角色中提取评估标准，构建可审计的评分系统。该系统能够验证成对偏好并提供用于强化学习的可靠奖励信号。实验表明，MRRG在多个基准测试中优于单角色评分方法，并能有效提升开放性生成任务的性能。

item.title: 通用关键点检测模型GKDT与大规模数据集MegaKPT的构建
item.content:encoded:
提出GKDT模型与MegaKPT数据集，提升关键点检测能力。



摘要： 随着预训练视觉语言模型的发展，计算机视觉正从窄领域向开放领域转变。为支持多样化任务，本文提出一个包含130万多样本的大规模统一关键点数据集MegaKPT，并基于DINOv3开发了通用关键点检测模型GKDT。该模型支持视觉和文本提示，通过混合模态提示训练和动态重要性采样等策略提升性能。实验表明，GKDT在22个测试集上表现优异，多数类别准确率超过90%，具有广泛的应用前景。相关数据集、模型和代码已开源。

item.title: 通过信息不对称提升多智能体系统预测性能
item.content:encoded:
信息不对称设计提升多智能体系统预测准确性。



摘要： 本文探讨了多智能体系统在事件预测中的应用，指出现有方法忽视了智能体所接收信息的差异性。当所有智能体共享相同证据时，推理过程会退化为从众而非真正的信念修正。为此，作者提出信息不对称设计，将证据分为公开和私有部分，使每个智能体拥有独占知识，只能通过讨论传递。理论分析表明，这种设计可降低智能体间错误相关性，并在InfoDelphi框架中实现，该框架包含基于相关性的证据路由、基于理由的迭代讨论和置信度加权聚合。实验结果显示，InfoDelphi在PolyGym基准测试中比单智能体和多智能体基线模型表现更优，验证了信息多样性对有效多智能体推理的关键作用。

item.title: MemSyco-Bench：评估智能体系统中记忆诱发迎合现象的基准
item.content:encoded:
提出MemSyco-Bench基准，评估记忆对智能体决策的影响。



摘要： 文章指出，尽管记忆在现代大语言模型代理中至关重要，但其可能引发迎合问题，导致代理过度迎合用户而牺牲事实准确性或客观推理。现有记忆基准主要关注记忆的存储和检索，忽视了记忆对后续推理和决策的影响。为此，作者提出MemSyco-Bench，一个全面评估记忆诱发迎合现象的基准，涵盖五项任务，包括拒绝记忆作为事实证据、尊重记忆适用范围、解决记忆与客观证据的冲突等。相关资源已开源供社区使用。

item.title: FlexDepth：一种面向复杂驾驶场景的自监督单目深度估计模型
item.content:encoded:
FlexDepth提升复杂驾驶环境下的单目深度估计性能。



摘要： 本文提出FlexDepth，一种自监督单目深度估计模型，旨在提升复杂驾驶环境下的深度预测性能。该模型采用静态-动态解耦训练策略，独立评估静态背景与动态物体的置信度，并引入Scale-Driven Decoder实现高效特征融合与高精度深度图输出。实验表明，FlexDepth在多个基准测试中表现优异，且模型轻量，适合边缘设备部署。

item.title: 自组织共形预测提升区域覆盖率
item.content:encoded:
SOCP通过自组织映射提升区域覆盖率，减少预测偏差。



摘要： 本文提出自组织共形预测（SOCP），利用自组织映射（SOM）发现输入空间中的子群，并在测试时从最佳匹配单元或固定网格邻域中提取局部校准缓冲区。该方法适用于回归和分类任务，保持预测器和非共形分数不变。SOCP保证了最佳匹配单元的精确有效性，以及邻域缓冲区的固定检索有效性，中央单元的有效性受柯尔莫哥洛夫-斯米尔诺夫偏差项影响。实验表明，SOCP在多个基准数据集上显著减少了区域覆盖率差距，同时预测集大小增加有限，计算开销小。

item.title: 基于语言条件的3D人体运动预测方法ZGL
item.content:encoded:
ZGL通过语言描述提升3D人体运动预测性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种轻量级语言条件预测器ZGL，利用观察到的运动描述作为语义先验，同时保留强动力学模型作为主要运动来源。该方法仅渲染已知姿态，通过视觉-语言模型生成一句话描述，并使用冻结的CLIP-L文本塔编码，将其投影为少量条件令牌。这些令牌通过带有零门控的紧凑交叉注意力适配器注入到基于DCT的空间-时间Transformer中，使网络在初始化时与纯姿态基线完全相同，仅在语言有助于减少预测误差时才学习使用语言信息。实验表明，ZGL在Human3.6M数据集上优于现有运动预测基线，在CMUMocap数据集上也表现出良好的泛化能力。

item.title: 基于结构化图像表示的机器人策略学习方法
item.content:encoded:
SIR方法提升机器人策略的可解释性与性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于结构化图像表示（SIR）的机器人策略学习方法，通过场景图作为中间表示来增强策略的可解释性。该方法首先构建一个全连接图，再通过模块化学习生成任务相关的稀疏子图，从而提升决策过程的透明度。实验表明，该方法在RoboCasa数据集上的成功率优于传统图像基线方法。此外，分析发现稀疏图能有效揭示数据集中的偏差，如虚假相关性和位置偏倚。

item.title: 基于多模态框架的缺血性心脏病筛查方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出IDNet多模态框架提升IHD筛查效果。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种基于Color Fundus Photography（CFP）的缺血性心脏病（IHD）筛查方法，针对现有研究中公开基准数据不足和视网膜图像与临床变量融合效率低的问题，提出了IDNet多模态框架。该框架采用Cross-Modal Distillation Aggregator（CDA）模块，通过可学习查询逐步整合左眼、右眼图像及临床特征，缓解高维视觉特征与低维表格数据之间的不平衡问题。研究还构建了一个可复现的UK Biobank基准数据集，并在该数据集上验证了IDNet的有效性，结果表明其优于仅使用图像、仅使用临床数据或多种多模态基线模型的表现。

item.title: 小型语言模型在RAG系统中的生成性能研究
item.content:encoded:
小型语言模型在RAG系统中表现良好，可本地运行无需GPU。



摘要： 本文研究了小型语言模型在检索增强生成（RAG）系统中的生成性能。尽管大型语言模型备受关注，但小型模型在多个领域仍具有重要价值。研究使用了多种开源和专有数据集进行基准测试，结果表明，小型语言模型可以在不依赖GPU的情况下，在设备上合理时间内完成RAG任务。实验代码和补充材料已发布在GitHub仓库中。

item.title: Goku数据集与Goku-Edit模型：推动视频编辑多任务发展
item.content:encoded:
Goku数据集支持多任务视频编辑，提升指令遵循能力。



摘要： 现有视频编辑数据集多聚焦于单任务外观编辑，难以满足真实场景的复杂需求。为此，研究者提出了Goku数据集，包含200万条高质量、指令对齐的视频编辑样本，首次将任务范围从基础外观编辑扩展到多任务和结构化操作。为应对复杂任务的数据合成挑战，研究设计了高效的数据合成流程，并引入渐进式过滤系统以确保数据可靠性。基于Goku，研究提出Goku-Edit模型，利用大语言模型作为文本编码器，采用解耦双分支结构提升编辑效果。同时构建了Goku-Bench基准测试，包含1000个人工验证的测试案例和7项新的编辑指标。在Goku-Bench上，Goku-Edit在指令遵循方面比其他开源模型提升了高达8%。

item.title: 基于物理约束的谐波分离框架用于腕部PPG的心率与呼吸率估计
item.content:encoded:
提出PCHS框架，提升运动状态下心率和呼吸率检测精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于物理约束的谐波分离（PCHS）框架，用于在自由活动条件下从腕部光电容积描记（PPG）信号中准确估计心率（HR）和呼吸率（RR）。该方法将HR和RR的估计转化为分析-合成问题，利用加速度计数据来分离运动伪影而非直接回归生理指标。通过物理引导的谐波生成器，将信号分解为准周期性生理成分和运动相关残差，从而实现HR和RR的可靠提取。实验表明，PCHS在运动密集型数据集上优于现有方法，并能提供可解释的信号分解结果。

item.title: 协作多智能体强化学习中的角色语义与协调结构研究
item.content:encoded:
研究角色语义与实际协调结构的差异，提升MARL系统性能。



摘要： 本文探讨了角色语义先验与协作多智能体强化学习（MARL）中实际形成的协调结构之间的差距。通过结合角色路由矩阵、形成敏感性指标（Δ_max）以及梯度/遮蔽归因方法，在三个角色的MiniGrid和SMACv2（Terran）环境中进行诊断分析。实验表明，基于标签的注意力机制在路由集中性和角色特异性方面优于平 MLP 基线，且在不同规模团队间具有零样本迁移能力。研究结果为评估MARL中的协调结构提供了实证框架，而非新的均衡概念或因果解释。

item.title: 基于RECIST的轻量级3D病灶分割模型LETT-NeXt
item.content:encoded:
LETT-NeXt通过CT图像和RECIST标记预测3D病灶掩码，性能优异。



摘要： 本文介绍了LETT-NeXt，一种基于RECIST指导的轻量级模型，用于从CT图像和RECIST标记中预测3D病灶掩码。该模型通过提取RECIST中心区域，将RECIST线和端点作为提示通道与CT输入拼接，使用MedNeXt-v2编码器解码生成病灶掩码，并结合提示感知组件选择和自适应AutoZoom推理。在公开验证集上，LETT-NeXt取得了79.4±10.1的Dice相似系数和72.3±16.2的归一化表面Dice值；在隐藏测试集上，DSC为73.9，NSD为67.3，挑战得分为70.6%。此外，其CPU推理速度为每例6.9±3.0秒，峰值内存占用3.6GB。

item.title: 基于多任务学习的低光图像质量评估模型LEIQ-Assessor
item.content:encoded:
LEIQ-Assessor提升低光图像质量评估效果。



摘要： 本文提出LEIQ-Assessor，一种基于多任务学习的低光图像增强质量评估模型。该模型利用预训练的SigLIP2 Vision Transformer作为主干网络，同时预测整体MOS及六个感知子属性：亮度、色彩保真度、噪声水平、曝光质量、自然性和内容恢复。通过PLCC损失联合优化多个目标，提升了特征表示的质量。实验表明，LEIQ-Assessor在MLE基准测试中优于现有无参考IQA模型和手工特征描述符，并在QoMEX 2026比赛中获得第二名。

item.title: 基于概率方法的MDP因果识别学习算法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种用于MDP因果识别的可靠学习方法。



摘要： 本文研究了在未知马尔可夫决策过程（MDPs）中识别概率提升因果关系的方法。传统方法依赖于难以获取的可达概率，而本文提出了一种基于重启的MDP修改方法，将因果识别问题转化为两个条件可达性查询，无需依赖原始MDP的可达概率。该方法具备概率保证，证明了其正确性，并建立了样本复杂度边界。同时，开发了一个渐进式的学习与验证算法，能够逐步分类状态为因果、非因果或未定。实验结果表明该方法在两个基准测试中能够快速且可靠地识别PR因果。

item.title: SDPO在持续后训练中的表现与局限性分析
item.content:encoded:
SDPO在稳定场景中加速专业化，但泛化能力差且易遗忘。



摘要： 本文研究了自蒸馏策略优化（SDPO）在持续后训练中的效果。实验表明，在教师信号稳定且对齐的情况下，SDPO能加速领域内专业化，但在分布外场景中表现不佳，容易导致遗忘甚至崩溃。与基于策略的强化学习方法如GRPO相比，SDPO表现出更强的遗忘倾向。进一步分析发现，密集自蒸馏会导致参数空间和响应空间的漂移，并通过师生循环放大高频格式错误。这些结果表明，仅依靠策略数据不足以实现有效的持续学习。当教师目标稳定且令牌级监督可靠时，密集自蒸馏可加速专业化，但不应作为持续后训练的默认稳定机制。

item.title: 基于查询适配的电信问答RAG系统优化研究
item.content:encoded:
研究查询适配RAG在电信问答中的性能提升方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了在电信问答任务中，通过查询端检索器适配而非生成器微调来提升检索增强生成（RAG）系统的可行性。研究指出，在参数受限和软检索假设下，查询编码器调优可能比监督微调更优。文章提出ARMOR方法，结合RAG似然和InfoNCE对比目标，优化检索效果并提升问答性能。实验表明，ARMOR在多个电信领域基准测试中表现优异。

item.title: 基于图的心脏分割与时间一致性优化方法
item.content:encoded:
通过时间正则化提升心脏分割的时序一致性与临床应用价值。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于图的自监督时间正则化方法，用于改善心脏分割的时序一致性。该方法在不依赖逐帧标注的情况下，通过惩罚速度和加速度的不连续性，实现更稳定的分割结果。同时，利用解剖对应关系自动映射到AHA 17段标准，支持标准化区域评估与病理性心肌运动检测。实验验证了该方法在临床中的有效性。

item.title: 基于克里金与神经网络的穿孔板压降预测模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出两种数据驱动模型预测穿孔板压降，性能优于传统公式。



摘要： 本文提出两种基于克里金方法和神经网络的数据驱动模型，用于预测湍流中圆形穿孔板的压降。模型利用文献中的两组实验数据进行训练，并与常用经验公式进行比较。结果表明，所提模型在大多数穿孔板配置中表现更优，且预测值与实验测量结果吻合良好。尽管训练数据有限，但两种方法仍具有可行性。为进一步验证其适用性，采用RANS方程模拟二维通道流动，将新模型作为动量方程中的源项引入，模拟结果与模型预测高度一致，证实了该方法在计算流体力学中的适用性。

item.title: 基于李群扩散的量子电路合成方法
item.content:encoded:
利用李群扩散进行硬件感知的量子电路合成。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于生成模型的量子电路合成方法，结合了单量子比特门在SU(2)流形上的连续性与纠缠电路结构的离散性。该方法包含两个组件：电路骨架选择器和扩散模型，用于在给定电路模板上生成符合硬件约束的量子门。实验展示了该方法在物理驱动的三量子比特哈密顿量模拟任务中的优越性能，并验证了其在不同门旋转角度下的可定制性。此外，研究还表明电路选择器能平衡电路复杂度与保真度，而非仅选择最复杂的模板。结果表明，李群扩散为硬件感知的量子电路合成提供了一个自然的生成框架。

item.title: SAGO：无需预处理的实时3D高斯分割框架
item.content:encoded:
SAGO实现3D高斯场景的实时交互分割，提升50倍速度。



摘要： 本文提出SAGO，一种无需预处理的实时3D高斯分割框架。传统方法需要耗时数秒甚至数分钟的预处理阶段，而SAGO通过引入虚拟无人机，将3D分割问题转化为在线最佳视角规划任务，从而实现亚秒级分割。实验表明，SAGO能直接从3D高斯中提取干净的3D资产，适用于物体操作和场景编辑等下游应用，并相比之前的方法提升了50倍速度。

item.title: 基于MPPCA的归一化流模型优化
item.content:encoded:
使用MPPCA作为潜在分布提升归一化流的训练效率和生成质量。



摘要： 归一化流是一种强大的生成模型，通过可逆映射将复杂数据分布与简单潜在分布相匹配。然而，传统选择的标准正态分布可能导致潜在空间与数据分布之间存在拓扑不匹配，影响训练效率和生成性能。本文提出使用混合概率主成分分析器（MPPCA）作为潜在分布，使潜在分布更贴近数据分布，从而加快收敛并提升生成效果。MPPCA可通过期望最大化算法快速拟合，适用于高维生成任务。实验表明，该方法在表格和图像数据集上均优于基线模型。

item.title: 基于hamm-grams的恶意软件检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出hamm-grams特征提升恶意软件检测效果。



摘要： 本文探讨了恶意软件检测中静态特征的局限性，提出了基于hamm-grams的新型特征构造算法。hamm-grams是一种具有固定长度和单字符通配符的正则表达式，通过设计一种新的局部敏感哈希算法，能够在小汉明距离的样本间产生碰撞，并利用哈希桶内的聚类来放置通配符。实验表明，该方法在恶意软件分类与检测任务中表现出显著优势。

item.title: AI预测系统通过模型集成提升准确性
item.content:encoded:
AI预测系统通过组合多样模型提升准确性。



摘要： 本文研究如何通过模型集成提高AI预测系统的准确性。尽管顶级AI预测系统已接近超级预测者的水平，但仍主要依赖现成的大型语言模型（LLMs）结合特定上下文收集和结构化处理。研究发现，仅靠增加样本数量并不足以提升准确率，因为许多前沿LLMs的预测高度相关，难以提供额外价值。相反，最佳集成模型是那些准确度高且预测差异大的模型，如Grok 4，因其预测与其他模型关联性较低。这表明，AI预测系统的强大之处在于组合具有互补误差的模型，而非盲目增加预测数量。

item.title: 基于自适应强化学习的水面自主车辆轨迹跟踪方法
item.content:encoded:
一种无需微调即可跨平台部署的自适应强化学习轨迹跟踪方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种自适应强化学习方法，用于水面自主车辆的轨迹跟踪，实现了零样本跨平台部署。该方法通过教师-学生架构，利用交互历史推断平台动力学的潜在表示，使策略在未知动力学条件下仍能有效工作。策略在模拟环境中训练，并直接应用于两个真实平台，无需微调。实验表明，该方法在位置均方误差上比非自适应方法提高58%，接近专用控制器的跟踪精度。

item.title: 长上下文下的模型退化现象与缓解策略研究
item.content:encoded:
长上下文导致模型性能下降，本文提出缓解策略。



摘要： 随着大语言模型在长周期任务中的广泛应用，长上下文带来的模型性能下降问题（即上下文退化）成为关键挑战。本文聚焦深度搜索场景，通过评估四个主流开源模型在三个基准上的表现，揭示了长上下文会导致模型提前放弃或给出不确定答案的现象，并发现该问题随上下文增长而加剧。通过剪枝实验，验证了上下文积累与退化之间的关系。此外，研究还探讨了通过上下文管理和后处理拒绝采样来缓解这一问题的方法。上下文管理方面，系统评估了七种不同方法，为策略选择提供指导；拒绝采样方面，提出了基于退化的过滤策略，并验证其有效性。最终，两种方法结合可进一步提升性能。

item.title: 基于隐式神经表示的无训练数据图像恢复框架MG-SpaIR
item.content:encoded:
MG-SpaIR通过多级粗到细残差结构实现高质量图像恢复。



摘要： MG-SpaIR是一种无需训练数据的图像恢复框架，能够从受模糊、下采样、噪声和缺失像素影响的单张图像中恢复出清晰图像。该方法基于隐式神经表示（INRs），引入了多级粗到细的残差层次结构，逐步提升重建精度并缓解频谱限制。同时，通过在高分辨率图像域应用显式稀疏近似正则化（如ℓ0型），有效抑制INR引起的伪影，保留锐利结构。优化过程采用多级近似交替方案，并在标准正则条件下保证收敛性。实验表明，MG-SpaIR在混合退化基准上优于现有无训练数据方法，提供了一种稳定、可解释且数据高效的图像恢复方案。

item.title: 面向K-12教育的AI风险评估数据集与可解释性评估研究
item.content:encoded:
构建用于教育内容风险评估的AI数据集及模型验证。



摘要： 本文介绍了AIriskEval-edu-db2，一个用于训练和评估基于大语言模型的审计员的数据集，旨在实现K-12教育内容的可解释性教学风险评估。该数据集包含1,639条来自170个科学问答题的解释，涵盖科学、语文和社科领域，每道题包含人类教师的解释以及11种由LLM模拟教师角色生成的不同教学风险解释。研究提出了一套符合教育标准的风险评估框架，涵盖事实准确性、深度完整性、相关性、学生适配性和意识形态偏差五个维度，并添加了785条带有结构化可解释性标注的解释。通过半自动流程并经教师专家验证，最终进行了实验比较，评估在AIriskEval-edu-db2上微调的本地模型是否能接近或超越前沿模型，同时保障教育审计中的隐私安全。

item.title: From Accuracy to Visual Dependence: Auditing and Filtering Modality Collapse in Traffic VideoQA
item.content:encoded: High benchmark accuracy does not guarantee genuine use of visual evidence. We study this problem in traffic accident Video Question Answering (VideoQA), where correct answers should depend on scene-specific visual evidence but may instead be inferred from textual shortcuts. Through an audit of four public benchmarks, we find that several recent open-weight Vision-Language Models (VLMs) perform competitively, and sometimes better, without video input. On the MM-AU benchmark, removing video consistently improves accuracy, and adding more frames further degrades performance. To quantify visual dependence, we introduce two dataset-level diagnostics: Blind Gap, measuring above-chance text-only performance, and Visual Gain, measuring the marginal benefit of adding video. We further propose an instance-level Shortcut Score that combines text-only confidence with visual necessity signals, enabling continuous, training-free filtering of shortcut-prone questions. The resulting subsets reduce shortcut bias and improve visual grounding. Our findings reveal large differences in grounding quality across benchmarks and show that visually grounded evaluation, not just high accuracy, is essential in safety-critical VideoQA.

item.title: Cross-Temporal Sinhala OCR: Page-Level Adaptation and Diachronic Analysis
item.content:encoded: Sinhala is a morphologically rich abugida spoken by roughly 16 million people in Sri Lanka, and to date, there are no publicly available real-world datasets for page-level Sinhala OCR. All previous studies for assessing Sinhala OCR models have used artificially generated data. To bridge the gap, we introduce sinhala-ocr-lk-acts-1010, an annotated dataset of 1,010 page-level images and their transcriptions collected from Sri Lankan Legislative Acts published between 1981-1989 and 2000-2019, split into 707 training examples, 101 validation examples, and 202 testing examples. Three models based on deep learning-based visual language processing, namely DeepSeek-OCR V1, DeepSeek-OCR V2, and LightOnOCR-2-1B, are fine-tuned using QLoRA in 8 experiments conducted on consumer and cloud GPUs. LightOnOCR-2-1B is the top performer, achieving a CER of 1.05% across all test examples, outperforming state-of-the-art open-source OCR models such as Surya-OCR (8.84%) and Tesseract v5 (10.69%), as well as commercially available OCR models such as Google Document AI (2.06%). Our results suggest that LightOnOCR-2-1B outperforms other baselines on real-world OCR tasks and maintains consistent performance across all print periods, even when documents are severely degraded.

item.title: Object Aligner: A Configurable JSON Schema Similarity Score for Graphs, Applied to LLM Prompt Optimization
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) are often asked to produce JSON conforming to a fixed schema, powering information extraction, tool calling, agentic planning, and knowledge-graph construction. Measuring how closely an output matches a gold reference is essential yet surprisingly hard: exact match is brittle, text similarity ignores structure, and an LLM judge is expensive, opaque, and non-deterministic. We address this with Object Aligner (OA), an open-source Python library that scores two JSON objects deterministically by recursively aligning their trees (the Hungarian algorithm for unordered collections, sequence alignment for ordered ones) and awarding partial credit at the granularity the schema declares. The Object Aligner is configured entirely through a set of JSON Schema extensions, so adapting it to a new task involves annotating a schema rather than writing code. Complex structured data, however, are rarely flat trees: records may form graphs or hypergraphs keyed by arbitrary identifiers, breaking the assumptions of prior similarity metrics. Our central contribution, referential alignment, closes this gap by inferring a bijection between gold and candidate identifiers and scoring every reference through it, so the score is invariant to relabeling. Since recovering this bijection exactly is graph isomorphism, the Object Aligner approximates it with Weisfeiler-Leman color refinement. An order-sensitive sequence regime targets ranking and planning. Since the same alignment localizes every mismatch, the Object Aligner emits ranked repair suggestions at no extra cost. Used as a reward inside the GEPA prompt optimizer, Object Aligner helps or stays neutral across all datasets.

item.title: AB-RAG: Adaptive Budgeted Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Reliable Question Answering
item.content:encoded: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become the standard way to ground large language models in external knowledge, yet most systems retrieve a fixed number of passages for every question regardless of its difficulty. This wastes computation on easy questions, starves hard ones, and gives no signal for when a generated answer can be trusted. With a growing share of question answering systems built on top of commercial language model APIs, a method that can decide how much to retrieve, and how far to trust its own answers, without retraining the underlying model, is of clear practical value. This paper presents AB-RAG (Adaptive Budgeted Retrieval-Augmented Generation), a training-free and backbone-agnostic framework that generates an answer, estimates its confidence from a combination of three signals, and then decides whether to stop or to retrieve more evidence, subject to a fixed retrieval budget. The estimator combines the model's own certainty, the agreement between the answer and the evidence, and the variance of the retrieval scores. For models that expose token probabilities the certainty signal is read directly; for closed APIs it is approximated by self-consistency, so the method works without access to model internals. Across three backbones and two datasets, the central result is that the confidence estimate reliably separates correct from incorrect answers on every backbone, reaching a clean split of 57.6% against 0% Exact Match between high- and low-confidence answers on a factoid dataset. The adaptive policy improves accuracy on capable backbones, and the study reports its negative and nuanced findings honestly, including a confidence signal that proved unsuitable for short answers and a retrieval signal whose sign was found and corrected through measurement. The entire study was carried out on a single consumer laptop with only a few dollars of API spend.

item.title: Notes on generative modeling: flow matching, diffusion, optimal transport and Schr{ö}dinger bridge
item.content:encoded: These notes recapitulate the high level mathematical principles behind different techniques for generative modeling. I show the connections between optimal transport and standard techniques such as Schr{ö}dinger bridge and flow matching.

item.title: Safety Targeted Embedding Exploit via Refinement
item.content:encoded: Safety training for large language models (LLMs) is conducted predominantly in English, leaving uncertain how well safety mechanisms generalize to low-resource languages and mixed-language code-switching. We show that this creates an epistemic gap in which models confidently generate harmful responses for inputs that fall outside the distribution of their safety training. To study this phenomenon, we introduce STEER (Safety Targeted Embedding Exploit via Refinement), a gradient-guided attack that identifies words contributing most strongly to the model's refusal behavior and iteratively translates them into low-resource languages to suppress refusal while preserving harmful intent. Across six open-source 8B-parameter models, STEER achieves attack success rates of up to 93.0% on JailbreakBench and 96.7% on AdvBench, outperforming random code-switching and Greedy Coordinate Gradient (GCG). The resulting prompts also transfer to GPT-4o-mini, achieving a 35.5% attack success rate without requiring access to the target model, suggesting that the underlying weakness is not specific to a single architecture. These findings demonstrate that safety mechanisms aligned primarily on English cannot be assumed to generalize across multilingual inputs. We argue that improving multilingual safety requires broader coverage during alignment and mechanisms that explicitly detect and abstain on out-of-distribution inputs.

item.title: Rethinking the Role of Feature Engineering and Learning Strategies in Few-Shot Hidden Emotion Recognition
item.content:encoded: In this paper, we present the solution developed by our team, XInsight Lab, which achieved first place in Track 3 of the 4th EI-MIGA-IJCAI Challenge with a test accuracy of 0.76923. To address the challenge of weak and sparse implicit emotion evidence in long videos, this paper extends the winning solution from the previous competition and proposes a compact multi-modal temporal modeling framework. The framework integrates and evaluates the effects of multi-source features, including 2D/3D skeletons, facial expression Blendshapes, DINOv2/v3 vision foundation models, X-CLIP video features, and Gemini semantic priors. Architecturally, we propose a cross-attention mechanism that utilizes static pose features, denoted as Base, as the Query and dynamic micro-motion differential features, denoted as Offset, as the Key and Value. By capturing local relative velocities, this mechanism eliminates static biases related to individual body shape and identity. Concurrently, an adaptive pooling method based on Multiple Instance Learning is employed to extract instantaneous emotions while suppressing background noise in long sequences. Finally, the paper reveals the representation collapse phenomenon of general vision foundation models in micro-dynamic tasks, and analyzes the underlying mechanisms where networks fall into public-leaderboard-driven pseudo-generalization due to shortcut learning and rote memorization.

item.title: Persona-Trained Monte Carlo: Estimating Market-Outcome Distributions via Swarms of Persona-Conditioned Neural Policy Bots in a Limit Order Book
item.content:encoded: We propose Persona-Trained Monte Carlo (PTMC), a method for estimating distributions of market-outcome statistics by repeatedly simulating limit-order-book interaction among swarms of persona-conditioned neural-policy trading bots. Each run instantiates many bots sharing one trained policy network but conditioned on heterogeneous, individually sampled persona parameters drawn from a learned trader-heterogeneity distribution; the bots interact in a continuous double auction, and the resulting price path is one Monte Carlo sample. Repeating this over independent persona-population draws yields an ensemble from which a target market statistic is estimated. Randomness enters through persona draws, within-run action sampling, and optional exogenous shocks, not solely through price as in classical Monte Carlo. We distinguish PTMC from adjacent paradigms, including classical Monte Carlo, hand-coded agent-based models, single-agent reinforcement learning, and large-language-model-based generative agents. To justify the design, we survey cross-disciplinary foundations -- agent-based computational economics, market microstructure, behavioral finance, deep reinforcement learning, generative/LLM-based agents, news-driven trading, systemic risk, econophysics, and game theory -- connecting each literature to a specific design choice in the policy network, training data, or validation protocol. We formalize the PTMC estimator and its convergence properties, specify a candidate bot architecture and training objective, and propose a four-level validation methodology: stylized-fact matching, microstructure- and agent-level checks, and historical stress-test comparison against a zero-intelligence baseline. The framework is proposed but not implemented: we contribute a formal estimator, a cross-disciplinary design justification, and a validation roadmap, and conclude with open research questions.

item.title: SAD-GS: Learning Reliable 3D Semantic Gaussian Fields via Dynamic Geo-Semantic Anchoring
item.content:encoded: Open-vocabulary 3D semantic Gaussian field learning relies on multi-view 2D supervision, whose semantic targets and spatial assignments are often unreliable. Across varying viewpoints, view-dependent features cause semantic identity drift, while propagated tracker masks introduce boundary leakage and identity switches. Directly optimizing against these unreliable 2D targets forces the 3D representation to absorb multi-view contradictions, leading to severe error accumulation. To resolve this limitation, we propose SAD-GS, a framework for learning reliable 3D semantic Gaussian fields via dynamic geo-semantic anchoring. Specifically, Semantic Anchor Distillation (SAD) distills per-view visual embeddings into consensus text anchors to establish a viewpoint-invariant semantic identity. Concurrently, the Geo-Semantic Feedback Loop (GSFL) leverages the evolving 3D field to actively filter tracker anomalies and refine spatial mask assignments via a conservative three-gate update rule. Extensive evaluations on LERF-OVS, 3D-OVS, and Mip-NeRF360 show that SAD-GS consistently achieves the best overall performance in both open-vocabulary localization and semantic segmentation. These comprehensive improvements validate the effectiveness and robustness of dynamic geo-semantic anchoring for reliable 3D semantic Gaussian field learning.

item.title: Scaling Up Thermodynamic AI Models
item.content:encoded: Thermodynamic computing devices based on the Ising model show great promise for low-power AI inference and edge computing, but scalable methods for training large models for such hardware remain limited. Prior theory shows that the time-averaged behavior of high-temperature Gibbs-sampled Ising systems can implement feed-forward neural inference. We turn this theoretical correspondence into a scalable and purely backpropagation-based algorithm for training deep convolutional networks for thermodynamic inference on Ising machine hardware. Our image classification models achieve accuracies of 94.9% on CIFAR-10 and 76.0% on CIFAR-100 under binary Gibbs sampling. We then develop and experimentally validate a mathematical theory relating inference cost to accuracy and controlling autocorrelation times. Subsequently, we calculate asymptotic results showing that inference cost is bounded by a well-controlled tradeoff with performance and exhibit algorithms for computing optimal inference schedules. Finally, we discuss implications for hardware development and the future of high-temperature thermodynamic AI models.

item.title: Beyond Adam: SOAP and Muon for Faster, Label-Efficient Training of Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
item.content:encoded: Machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) have become a hallmark of AI for scientific simulation. While efforts on new architectures and datasets have led to increasingly accurate and general models, the choice of optimizer for training has largely remained unexplored, defaulting to Adam and its variants in the community. Here, we implement and systematically compare a class of recently proposed matrix-structured optimizers, including Muon, SOAP, and the hybrid SOAP-Muon, for training NequIP and Allegro MLIP models. We find that these optimizers can substantially outperform Adam in both convergence speed and final accuracy. SOAP and SOAP-Muon emerge as robust and consistently strong methods, while Muon only provides partial gains relative to Adam. The improvements are particularly pronounced under partial force supervision. Our results indicate that optimizer choice is an overlooked yet impactful design axis for MLIPs.

item.title: Seed2.0 Model Card: Towards Intelligence Frontier for Real-World Complexity
item.content:encoded: We present Seed2.0, a model series that takes a meaningful step toward solving complex, real-world tasks. Our approach begins with identifying users' genuine needs and constructing a reliable, forward-looking evaluation system by selecting and abstracting benchmarks grounded in these needs and in realistic, complex scenarios. Guided by this evaluation system, Seed2.0 targets two persistent challenges, long-tail knowledge and complex instruction following, substantially improving the model's reliability on intricate, long-horizon tasks. Beyond these, Seed2.0 delivers world-leading reasoning intelligence, visual understanding, and search capabilities that address the most common needs of a broad user base. Through extensive real-world use cases documented in this model card, we demonstrate that Seed2.0 begins to exhibit the ability to handle initial complex real-world tasks, delivering greater value to hundreds of millions of users.

item.title: The Verbose Context Problem in Medical Records
item.content:encoded: The verbose context problem occurs when structured concepts have token-inefficient textual representations. This bottleneck is acute in population health: cohort-level analysis of longitudinal patient records requires reasoning over thousands of medically-coded events, often exceeding 400K tokens in total. We present PopMedQA, a benchmark isolating this problem through computational tasks on groups of longitudinal patient records. We construct the benchmark using neopatient, a new library for language-controlled generation of artificial patient records. Through extensive ablations -- including prompting strategies, prompt compression, and agentic decomposition -- we find that domain-independent methods fail to alleviate the verbose context problem. There remains significant opportunity to exploit domain-specific structure in language model inputs for population-scale reasoning.

item.title: Bandwidth Selection in Kernel Density Estimation for Model Calibration
item.content:encoded: As deep learning models are increasingly deployed in high-stakes applications, providing well-calibrated uncertainty estimates has become as critical as achieving high predictive accuracy. While Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) has emerged as a smooth and continuous alternative to traditional binning for quantifying miscalibration, its reliability is heavily dependent on the choice of the kernel bandwidth. Standard selection techniques, such as Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE), often fail to produce optimal bandwidths for calibration tasks. In this work, we introduce Risk Alignment (RA), a novel optimization framework that determines the optimal bandwidth by aligning KDE-reconstructed risk with empirical risk. We theoretically demonstrate that this alignment minimizes calibration estimation bias across the data distribution, establishing a principled bandwidth selection criterion applicable to various metrics, including the challenging case of canonical calibration error. Extensive experiments across multiple architectures and datasets show that RA consistently outperforms standard bandwidth selection methods, yielding more reliable calibration assessments.

item.title: Adjusted Wasserstein distances for bridging empirical and true distributions with applications to MDS
item.content:encoded: This paper examines how metric adjustments to Multidimensional Scaling (MDS) can enhance its effectiveness as a visual tool for pattern recognition. The distance under consideration, referred to as Max-D-SW, is an adjustment of the Max-Sliced Wasserstein distance. In contrast to the original formulation, which optimizes over single unit directions, Max-D-SW aggregates contributions over orthonormal bases. This modification provides a clear numerical advantage in MDS outcomes, particularly when applied to heavy-tailed distributions. We also establish sample-complexity bounds showing that Max-D-SW remains statistically tractable, with rates comparable to those of its max-sliced counterpart. Moreover, we show that a better sample complexity for a metric does not necessarily translate into better performance when the metric is used as an input for MDS.

item.title: ReQuest: Rethinking-based Question-Aware Frame Selection for Long-Form Video QA
item.content:encoded: Recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have substantially advanced video understanding, yet long-form video QA remains challenging under fixed input token budgets, where uniform sampling can be inefficient for evidence localization. We propose ReQuest , an uncertainty-driven, question-adaptive keyframe selection pipeline that aligns question intent with relevant video content through selective computation. ReQuest integrates (i) a lightweight question-aware selector distilled from MLLM-generated supervision, (ii) Re-thinking Routing that triggers additional inference only when the model is uncertain with a length-adaptive criterion, and (iii) uncertainty-guided adaptive non-maximum suppression that selects temporally diverse frames while adjusting spacing based on question difficulty. As a plug-andplay method, ReQuest improves long-video QA without modifying or fine-tuning the underlying MLLM. Experiments on Video-MME, MLVU, and LongVideoBench demonstrate consistent accuracy gains with competitive computational cost, with particularly strong improvements in medium and long video regimes.

item.title: GaussianEmoTalker: Real-Time Emotional Talking Head Synthesis with Audio-Driven and Blendshape-Based 3D Gaussian Splatting
item.content:encoded: Audio-driven talking head synthesis has achieved impressive progress in lip synchronization and visual quality, yet generating expressive emotional avatars with controllable intensity remains challenging, especially under real-time constraints. In this paper, we present GaussianEmoTalker, an audio-driven framework for real-time emotional talking head synthesis based on 3D Gaussian Splatting. Instead of directly predicting the final emotional avatar from speech, we formulate emotional animation as a neutral-to-emotional residual deformation problem. GaussianEmoTalker first constructs an identity-specific neutral talking space with GaussianBlendshapes, which provides high-fidelity Gaussian attributes and phoneme-synchronized neutral motion. It then predicts an emotion-conditioned residual deformation by combining mesh displacement cues, audio features, emotion categories, and intensity encodings. To fuse these heterogeneous signals, we introduce a spatial-audio-emotion attention module that estimates the offsets of Gaussian attributes for expressive and temporally stable rendering. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GaussianEmoTalker achieves competitive video quality, accurate lip synchronization, controllable emotional expression, and real-time rendering compared with recent emotional talking head methods. Our project page is available at https://njust-yang.github.io/GaussianEmoTalker.github.io/

item.title: Revisiting Chain-of-Thought Reasoning under Limited Supervision: Semi-supervised Chain-of-Thought Learning
item.content:encoded: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning has emerged as an effective approach for activating latent reasoning capabilities in large language models. However, most existing CoT methods use reasoning chains mainly as inference-time prompts, while the generated reasoning traces are rarely reused as semi-supervised learning signals. In this report, we define \textbf{Semi-supervised Chain-of-Thought Learning} and propose \textbf{Semi-CoT}, a simple framework that uses unlabeled questions to construct pseudo reasoning supervision. Semi-CoT samples multiple pseudo-CoTs for each unlabeled question, estimates answer-level semantic entropy, and selects low-entropy reasoning chains as reliable pseudo-CoT demonstrations. This extends the self-training view of CoT from inference-time refinement to semi-supervised pseudo-supervision. Pilot experiments on AQuA, SVAMP, GSM8K, and MultiArith show that the entropy gate selects high-precision pseudo-CoTs, with pseudo-answer precision ranging from $91.36\%$ to $100\%$. Semi-CoT also gives small gains on SVAMP and GSM8K, while AQuA shows negative transfer and MultiArith reaches a ceiling. These results suggest that unlabeled questions can provide reliable pseudo reasoning signals, but their effective use still requires stronger demonstration selection or student training.

item.title: A Deep Multiscale Neural Network for Accurate Neurological Disorder Detection from MRI Scans and Real-Time Web Deployment
item.content:encoded: Neurological disorders involve diverse pathologies of the brain and nervous system, making early and accurate detection essential. While many deep CNNs have been developed for MRI-based classification of neurological disorders, most are optimized for binary tasks and often fail to capture the multi-class features needed to distinguish subtle anatomical differences across conditions. This study proposes the Enhanced Neurological Disorder Detection Network (End-Net) for multi-class MRI classification of neurological disorders. End-Net includes 24 convolutional layers, beginning with convolutional blocks followed by 21 optimized inception modules. These modules extract multiscale features via parallel 1 x 1, 3 x 3, and factorized 5 x 5 convolutional branches, along with max pooling, enabling the model to capture complementary texture, edge, shape, and contextual information. A global average pooling head, compact fully connected classifier, and dropout reduce parameters, limit overfitting, and improve robustness. End-Net was evaluated on the Multi-Class Neurological Disorder dataset, comprising MRI scans from patients with Alzheimer's disease, brain tumors, multiple sclerosis, and healthy controls. Severe class imbalance was addressed by augmenting minority classes with WGAN-GP and randomly undersampling the majority class. The results show that End-Net outperforms existing architectures in both accuracy and generalization. The model is also integrated into an online system for real-time web-based inference and accessibility.

item.title: 基于视觉语言模型的尼日利亚车牌识别研究
item.content:encoded:
VLM在尼日利亚车牌识别中表现优于传统方法。



摘要： 本文研究了视觉语言模型（VLM）在尼日利亚车牌识别中的应用，对比了多种VLM模型在真实复杂场景下的表现。通过88张挑战性图像测试，结果显示Gemini和Qwen在准确性和鲁棒性上显著优于其他模型。研究指出VLM相较于传统YOLO+OCR系统具有更低资源需求和更强环境适应能力，同时质疑部分模型供应商的性能宣称。

item.title: 基于提示的定向声音定位方法SelectTSL
item.content:encoded:
提出SelectTSL模型实现精准目标声音定位。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于提示的定向声音定位方法SelectTSL，旨在解决现有深度学习系统在复杂声学场景中无法选择性定位目标声音的问题。该方法通过Prompt-Guided Selective Attention Module生成提示引导的嵌入向量，结合相位差增强模块和目标幅度信息，联合估计到达方向和目标源数量。实验表明，该方法在合成数据和真实录音中均优于其他基线模型，具有良好的泛化能力。

item.title: 混合量子-经典神经网络在情感分析中的应用
item.content:encoded:
混合模型在情感分析中表现优异，具备更强的泛化能力。



摘要： 本文研究了混合量子-经典神经网络在情感分析中的应用，特别是在与新冠疫情相关的推文数据集上。通过将文本向量化为TF-IDF特征，并输入到经典前馈网络和混合架构中进行训练，结果表明混合模型在准确率上可与经典模型媲美，且在验证损失和准确率方面表现出不同的学习动态，暗示其具有更丰富的表示能力。此外，在短信垃圾分类任务中，混合模型的准确率提升了15个百分点，显示出更好的泛化性能。这表明量子机器学习在自然语言处理中的可行性及混合模型的优势。

item.title: 基于文本驱动的3D手物交互生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种单阶段扩散框架，提升手物交互的物理合理性。



摘要： 本文研究了文本驱动的3D手物交互生成问题，针对现有方法在物理合理性方面的不足，提出了一种单阶段扩散框架JointHOI。该框架同时生成手部动作和动态接触图，通过将接触作为辅助模态，使模型在训练中学习接触与动作的耦合关系。推理时，接触引导采样确保生成的接触图与动作几何一致，从而提高时间稳定性并减少穿透和漂浮现象。实验表明，该方法在GRAB和ARCTIC数据集上均优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于语义特征组的多变量时间序列分类器反事实解释框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于语义组的反事实生成框架，提升康复运动分析的可解释性。



摘要： 本文针对多变量时间序列分类器在康复运动分析中的反事实解释问题，提出一种基于语义特征组的两阶段框架。传统方法通常在通道层面生成反事实解释，导致结果分散且生物力学不一致。作者首先验证了Shapley-Adaptive组排序方法的有效性，但发现其无法保证组级稀疏性。随后引入Learnable Gate方法，通过可学习的组相关性门控和扰动掩码联合优化，显著提升了模态组稀疏性，同时保持解释的有效性、时间平滑性和生成效率。实验表明，该方法能提供更符合临床推理的肌肉级修正指导。

item.title: LLM生成代码与注释的特征及影响研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM生成代码和注释在软件开发中广泛应用，但存在调试复杂性和注释不自然等问题。



摘要： 本文研究了LLM生成的代码和注释的特征，以及其在公司和社区维护仓库中的差异。通过分析2021至2025年的活跃仓库数据，发现LLM生成代码的比例随时间下降，且多出现在测试用例中，而注释则保持稳定。研究还发现，LLM生成代码具有较高的代码克隆现象，而注释语法正确率较低。公司维护的仓库中LLM生成内容比例更高，但仅有少量人工标注的bug与LLM生成代码相关。

item.title: 基于Haar小波和t检验的时间序列异常检测算法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型无监督时间序列异常检测方法，效果优于现有基准。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种基于Haar离散小波变换和设计的t检验的新型无监督异常检测算法。针对实际应用中异常样本稀少和标注数据不足的问题，该算法无需标签即可有效检测异常。通过在343个数据集上的广泛实验，验证了其在无监督和自监督基准中的优越性能。研究强调了该方法在处理高误报率问题上的潜力，尤其适用于安全关键型应用场景。

item.title: 基于动态系统学习的3D关节物体生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出PWM-ArtGen模型，提升3D关节物体生成精度与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文针对单图像生成3D关节物体时难以准确预测运动结构的问题，提出一种基于动态系统的统一模型PWM-ArtGen。该模型通过联合学习视觉动态与运动参数分布，结合动作扩散和图像扩散机制实现无标注数据的协同训练。研究还构建了一个包含19.7万对部分级图像的逼真数据集，用于支持模型训练。实验表明，该方法在静态状态下表现优于现有基线，并具备强大的零样本泛化能力，适用于复杂现实场景中的物体生成。

item.title: 全球图书馆与信息科学研究方法的国家差异分析
item.content:encoded:
研究显示各国LIS研究方法存在显著差异，且随时间逐渐缩小。



摘要： 本文通过分析过去30年间来自81个国家的5281篇国际期刊论文，探讨了图书馆与信息科学（LIS）研究方法的国家差异。研究采用人工标注与深度学习模型结合的方式，对研究方法进行自动分类，并进行了跨国比较。结果显示，不同国家在研究方法的使用上存在明显差异，且这些差异在近30年有所缩小。研究还指出，即使针对相同主题，各国的研究方法也可能不同，这反映了各国学科发展的独特路径。该研究为理解各国学科发展特征、促进国际合作提供了重要参考。

item.title: 无需训练的新型视图视频合成方法NeoMap
item.content:encoded:
NeoMap在不训练模型的情况下实现高质量视图合成。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为NeoMap的新框架，用于从单张图像或单目视频中生成新颖视角的视频。传统方法依赖于相机条件、任务特定微调或硬去噪指导，常导致伪影和场景一致性下降。NeoMap通过在预训练视频模型的自然数据流形中定位高保真、视角一致的解决方案，无需额外训练即可实现更优效果。实验表明，NeoMap在Tanks-and-Temples、LLFF和DAVIS等基准测试中均优于现有方法，达到了最先进的生成质量和视角一致性。

item.title: 滚动分拆共形预测在牵引控制中的应用评估
item.content:encoded:
研究显示滚动分拆共形预测无法有效预警牵引力损失。



摘要： 本文评估了滚动分拆共形预测作为牵引力损失前兆信号的可行性。通过分析19名车手的55,563个测试阶段遥测数据，发现该方法在14个真实事故事件中平均精确率和召回率均为0，且误报率高达15.3%，表明其不适用于早期预警。此外，研究指出模型的独立性假设被违反，导致高误报率。研究结果为负面结论，并探讨了改进方向。

item.title: 基于深度学习的电路图转网表系统SINA
item.content:encoded:
SINA实现高精度电路图转网表，提升EDA效率。



摘要： 本文介绍SINA，一种用于将电路图图像转换为机器可读网表的开源系统。SINA通过深度学习进行元件检测、连通性分析、OCR提取参考设计器，并利用视觉语言模型进行参考设计器分配，有效解决模拟和混合信号领域中电路知识难以机器处理的问题。系统支持IC和PCB级电路图，具备交叉导线检测功能，实验显示其网表生成准确率达96.67%，显著优于现有方法。

item.title: HECATE：评估LLM集成应用中提示与代码层复杂度的工具
item.content:encoded:
HECATE首次评估LLM应用中提示与代码的复杂度。



摘要： 本文介绍了HECATE，一款用于评估LLM集成应用中提示层和代码层复杂度的工具。该工具基于Prompt-as-Specification方法，将每个提示视为行为规范，并从25个复杂度维度生成52个候选指标。通过分析18个开源仓库中的118个组件，筛选出10个有效指标，其中7个为新引入的结构广度指标，如LLM调用点、内存属性等。研究还发现，提示层复杂度在考虑代码复杂度后仍具有显著性，证明其作为独立维度的重要性。最终验证显示，最佳指标在6个外部仓库中仍能有效预测维护工作量。

item.title: 基于单张全景X光片生成3D牙科体积的X-Splat方法
item.content:encoded:
X-Splat通过单张全景X光片生成高精度3D牙科体积，优于传统方法。



摘要： 本文提出X-Splat，一种基于高斯点云的框架，用于从单张全景X光片（PXR）生成类似CBCT的3D牙科体积。该方法利用已知的全景成像几何结构作为生成基础，通过可学习的各向异性高斯基元沿X射线路径进行初始化，并通过Beer-Lambert重投影和多视角放射学训练监督进行调整。轻量级残差修正器引入数据集级别的解剖先验，同时不干扰高斯基元已解析的几何信息。实验使用合成PXR-CBCT对进行训练，实现直接的体素监督。此外，引入基于分割的几何感知指标，首次对基于PXR的生成方法进行评估。X-Splat在重建个体牙齿、皮质边界和牙槽结构方面表现优异，包括下颌管等以往方法无法重建的结构。

item.title: 基于Ghost变量与Shapley值的时序模型滞后相关性分析方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种衡量时序预测模型滞后相关性的方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种与模型无关的方法，用于测量机器学习时序预测模型中的滞后相关性。该方法结合了Ghost变量、Shapley值和加法重要性度量，引入了自相关性和部分自相关性函数作为滞后重要性指标。此外，还提出了一种新的方法，用同一模型的一步预测来替代联盟方法中缺失的特征。通过不同模拟和真实数据案例进行评估，结果表明该框架在大多数情况下成功展示了预期的滞后结构。研究使用了季节性ARMA模型和循环神经网络进行验证。

item.title: DRDN：解决类增量学习中共享表示退化问题的新方法
item.content:encoded:
DRDN通过解耦表示和任务令牌扩展提升类增量学习性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为DRDN的新型类增量学习方法，旨在解决长期任务序列中共享表示退化和跨任务混淆的问题。DRDN通过在每个增量步骤中持续应用掩码图像建模（MIM），仅通过共享骨干网络传递重建梯度，从而增强其对通用视觉结构的保留能力。同时，采用分层任务令牌扩展机制，结合改进的每任务注意力规则，减少任务间干扰。实验表明，在无外部预训练的ViT设置下，DRDN在CIFAR100-B0数据集上取得了77.19%的平均准确率，优于现有基线方法，且性能优势随任务数量增加而扩大。

item.title: WattGPU：基于元数据和规格的LLM推理能耗与延迟预测模型
item.content:encoded:
WattGPU通过公开数据预测LLM推理能耗和延迟，无需硬件访问。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为WattGPU的新型预测框架，用于估算大型语言模型（LLM）在不同GPU上的平均功耗和令牌间延迟。该方法仅依赖于公开的LLM元数据和GPU规格，无需实际硬件访问或性能基准测试，具有良好的泛化能力。研究使用了42个开源LLM和8种GPU进行评估，结果表明，在未见过的GPU上，功耗预测误差不超过3.4%（离线场景）和13.5%（服务器场景），延迟预测误差不超过8.5%。相比传统基线模型，WattGPU显著提高了预测精度，适用于优化LLM部署。

item.title: 后校准在语义分割中的可靠性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究后校准对语义分割模型置信度估计的影响与改进方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了在语义分割中，后校准技术如何影响模型的置信度估计，并分析了两个关键问题：一是不同logits表示可能导致不同的校准结果，二是校准目标与训练目标不一致可能破坏分割结果。作者提出了翻译不变校准器和决策保持校准方法，以提高校准效果并避免分割性能下降。实验表明，这些方法在多个基准数据集上有效提升了校准指标并保持了良好的分割性能。

item.title: 基于神经引导的视频合成框架探索动态视觉选择性
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新框架生成优化视频刺激，研究大脑视觉区域动态处理机制。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于神经引导的视频合成框架，用于生成优化的动态视频刺激，以探索大脑视觉皮层不同区域的动态视觉选择性。该方法通过进化搜索在结构化提示空间中寻找能最大化目标脑区活动的视频内容，并利用动态编码模型预测脑区响应。实验表明，合成视频能够揭示视觉通路中对时间动态敏感性的系统差异，并揭示侧向流中社会动态特征的复杂性进展。该框架为动态视觉选择性的计算研究提供了新工具，有助于指导未来的在体实验。

item.title: TTT-PLC：一种自监督测试时调优的音频包丢失隐藏方法
item.content:encoded:
TTT-PLC通过自监督方式适应性提升音频包丢失隐藏效果。



摘要： 本文提出TTT-PLC，一种基于自监督测试时调优的音频包丢失隐藏框架。该方法利用已接收的音频信号生成监督信号，对现有PLC模型进行适应性调整，无需干净参考信号或外部数据。研究在非因果和因果两种部署场景中验证了该方法的有效性，表明预训练PLC模型在推理时不应视为固定，已接收的信号片段可有效用于提升同一信号的隐藏性能。

item.title: 基于声调泄露的语音分类后门攻击研究
item.content:encoded:
提出TLA攻击方法，提升语音模型后门攻击效果与隐蔽性。



摘要： 本文探讨了当前语音分类模型在深度神经网络防御下的脆弱性，并引入了一种名为Timbre Leakage Attack（TLA）的新型后门攻击方法。该方法通过在深度自监督特征中传播声调信息，生成看似自然的中毒样本。同时，文中还提出了Pmeta-TLA训练机制，结合元学习和投影冲突梯度（PCGrad）实现多后门一次性嵌入。实验表明，该策略在关键词识别任务中表现出更高的攻击效果、更强的隐蔽性和鲁棒性，且攻击成本更低。

item.title: 基于AC-SLSIoU的红外小目标检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出AC-SLSIoU提升红外小目标检测精度与边界质量。



摘要： 红外小目标检测面临目标尺寸小、信噪比低、背景干扰严重等问题。现有方法依赖概率域监督，难以区分弱目标与强噪声。本文提出AC-SLSIoU损失函数，通过Logit域边缘约束增强弱目标识别，自适应边界抑制优化目标轮廓，并引入误报焦点损失减少高置信度误检。该方法无需额外推理开销，可无缝集成至现有检测器，显著提升检测准确性和形状质量。实验验证了其有效性、鲁棒性与泛化能力。

item.title: 基于神经元激活的无标注自蒸馏方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出Neuron-OPSD框架提升模型在专业领域的性能。



摘要： 本文针对缺乏真实交互反馈或人工标注监督的大型语言模型训练难题，提出一种无需标注的自蒸馏方法Neuron-OPSD。该方法通过内部神经元激活指导训练数据选择和教师上下文构建，利用在线蒸馏进行训练，无需任何真实标签。实验表明，该框架在专业领域任务中提升了性能，同时保持了跨领域泛化能力并缓解了校准崩溃问题，适用于无法进行在线交互或外部监督的场景。

item.title: SemHash LLM：高效语义去重框架
item.content:encoded:
SemHash LLM 提供高效语义去重，提升文档重复检测质量。



摘要： SemHash LLM 是一种多粒度文档去重框架，结合了语义投影哈希、注意力加权 MinHash、对比边界学习和选择性 LLM 验证。该方法通过门控融合字符、词和文档级信号，并采用级联过滤管道实现高效的候选文档减少。语义投影哈希在精简的 LLM 嵌入空间中生成紧凑二进制代码，而注意力加权 MinHash 有效抑制冗余内容并强调信息部分。自适应决策边界和不确定性估计进一步提升了对模板污染、短文本扰动、包含关系和病毒片段等场景的鲁棒性。实验表明，SemHash LLM 在不到 1% 的神经网络验证成本下实现了强大的重复检测效果。

item.title: Web UI图像变化描述任务与基准数据集研究
item.content:encoded:
提出WUICC任务及基准，提升UI差异检测准确性。



摘要： 本文介绍了视觉回归测试（VRT）在软件发布流程中的重要性，并指出传统像素级比较方法存在大量误报问题。为解决这一问题，作者提出了Web UI图像变化描述任务（WUICC），并发布了首个相关数据集和基准。通过评估多种图像差异描述方法和零样本大语言模型，发现这些方法在Web UI领域面临布局多样性、密集文本和细微变化等挑战，但相比传统方法能更有效地过滤无意义的视觉噪声，为未来研究奠定基础。

item.title: HOLA：结合线性注意力与记忆缓存的语言模型优化方法
item.content:encoded:
HOLA通过引入记忆缓存提升语言模型的长序列处理能力。



摘要： HOLA是一种基于线性注意力机制的语言模型优化方法，借鉴了互补学习系统的理念。它在传统的线性注意力基础上增加了一个有限的精确键值缓存，形成半参数化的测试时记忆系统。该缓存通过预测残差选择存储内容，并通过解耦的RMSNorm-gamma机制提高检索精度。实验表明，HOLA在Wikitext和LAMBADA任务上表现优于全注意力Transformer++，并在长序列回忆任务中表现出更强的鲁棒性。

item.title: 社交平台虚假信息核查系统中的合成共识攻击分析
item.content:encoded:
研究社交平台虚假信息核查系统中合成共识的潜在风险与应对措施。



摘要： 本文探讨了社交平台如X、Meta等采用的众包事实核查系统，特别是基于矩阵分解的桥梁算法。研究发现，恶意用户可通过策略性评分制造虚假共识，影响核查结果。利用历史数据，研究显示不到10次评分即可操纵10.7%的低质量标注。此外，研究还揭示了“不帮助”评分可能提升内容的有用性评分，并提出了成本模型来量化攻击难度。为应对这一问题，X已在其社区备注系统中部署缓解措施。

item.title: AUF改进块解码器的生成效率与准确性
item.content:encoded:
AUF提升块解码器生成长度与性能。



摘要： 本文提出AUF方法，通过调整损失函数的监督方式，提高块解码器在生成过程中的准确性和效率。传统方法在训练时对整个块进行监督，而AUF仅关注已接受前缀的损失，从而优化了训练效果。实验表明，在多个基准测试中，AUF显著提升了平均生成长度，并且在不同架构上均表现出色。此外，研究还发现仅依赖衰减的基线模型虽然在块内准确率较高，但在解码性能上表现不佳。

item.title: FlowCIR：一种高效的零样本组合图像检索方法
item.content:encoded:
FlowCIR通过语义传输实现高效零样本图像检索，提升负向指令处理能力。



摘要： 本文提出FlowCIR，一种基于条件语义传输的零样本组合图像检索方法。与传统依赖文本反转的方法不同，FlowCIR通过学习轻量级的运输场，将指令表示映射到与目标对齐的查询嵌入中，无需更新图像或文本编码器，从而提高计算效率。该方法在标准CIR基准测试中表现出色，并针对VLM在处理否定指令时的不足，引入了多否定引导策略，显著提升了负向查询的鲁棒性。
